name: scriptorium-mundane
summary: A secular copy-house or early print shop. Rows of copy desks with scribes hunched over, ink-wells, a printing press if advanced, ledgers bound for merchants and courts.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle, desks oriented toward the light source

primary_palette.note: classic scribal hall — three copy desks facing the windows
print shop — press central, compositor at the type case
master-reader scriptorium — lectern reads aloud, scribes copy in parallel

story_prompts: deadline — every desk occupied, master pacing, a merchant waiting at the door
forgery — one scribe hiding a document as the master approaches
ink-shipment late — scribes sharpening quills, nothing to do
night copy — single scribe, single candle, a forbidden text on the desk

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: steady reading light (0.5) — scribes need clean light

anti_patterns: desks face the light source — never placed with backs to windows
keep the press away from shelved parchment — ink splatter hazard
inkwells belong on desks, not on the floor — they're implied, not decorative
